description_long: This object is a pair of pliers with a robust, utilitarian design, featuring two elongated, slightly curved handles that converge at a central pivot. The handles are made of a dark, metallic material and lack any visible insulation or ergonomic padding, giving them a raw, industrial appearance. The jaws are flat and broad, with squared-off tips, suitable for gripping, bending, or compressing materials. The pivot mechanism is a simple fixed joint, allowing the jaws to open and close in a scissor-like motion. The surface of the pliers is matte and slightly rough, suggesting a forged or cast metal construction. No visible branding or decorative elements are present, emphasizing function over form.
description: A sturdy, dark metal pair of pliers with curved handles and flat, squared jaws, featuring a simple fixed pivot and a utilitarian finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved handles and the flat, squared jaws.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the open jaws and the symmetrical curvature of the handles.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handles' length and the alignment of the jaws.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the handles' curvature and the back of the pivot joint.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, straight metal handles without insulation or ergonomic features.
attribute_jaw_design: Flat, squared jaws with no visible serration or wire-cutting feature, designed for general gripping.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Simple fixed pivot joint allowing standard scissor-like motion, non-adjustable.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metal surface with no visible grip pattern or texturing.
